blocks.content:
In this first lesson, you learn how to start a conversation, say your name, the alphabet and 
spelling, ask simple questions, answer about yourself, and make a short introduction in English.

blocks.title: Conversation Model
blocks.instruction: Use a short answer first. Then you can add extra information.

messages.speaker: Emma
messages.text: My name is Emma Watson. What's your name?

messages.speaker: Emma
messages.text: My friends are calling me, bye bye.

cards.backText: Use these expressions depending on the time of day.
cards.shortMeaning: Use these expressions depending on the time of day.

cards.backText: Use these expressions to say your name.
cards.shortMeaning: Use these expressions to say your name.

cards.backText: Use this question to ask a person's name.
cards.shortMeaning: Use this question to ask a person's name.

cards.backText: Use these expressions when you meet a person for the first time.
cards.shortMeaning: Use these expressions when you meet a person for the first time.

cards.backText: Use these expressions to finish a conversation.
cards.shortMeaning: Use these expressions to finish a conversation.

blocks.text:
[] you study?

Yes, I [].
No, I [].

blocks.objective: Play the dialog above, change the names and expressions.
blocks.tips: Try changing the expressions and the names of the characters.

blocks.content: There are 26 letters in the English alphabet.

blocks.instruction: In this exercise you are Sophia. Answer the questions

blocks.content: Speeling is act of saying letters individually to form names or words.
Now it's your turn, practice following the example!

prompts: What is your opinion on the topic?

blocks.content:
To make simple direct questions in the present, we use "**Do + person + action?**". 
We can ask about actions, routines, and preferences.

blocks.title: Basic Structure
blocks.details: We use DO combined with I, You, They and We

blocks.title: More ways to answer
blocks.explanation: Use a short answer first. Then you can add extra information.
blocks.details: Keep the same answer pattern every time in this part.

cells.text: Yes, I do. I study at night.
No, I don't. I study in the morning.
Yes, I do. I like pop music.

blocks.text:
[] you study?

Yes, I [].
No, I [].

Do you [] English?
Do you [] music?
Do you [] games?
Do you [] movies?

blocks.title: Listen and Answer
blocks.instruction: In this exercise you are Sofia. Answer the questions

script.speaker: Lucas
script.text: Hi Sofia! Do you study English?

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I study at night.

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: No, I don't. I don't have time to.

script.speaker: Sofia
script.text: Yes, I do. I like rock.

script.speaker: Lucas
script.text: Cool! Let's go to a show someday...

options.text: Yes, I do. I like reggae.
No, I don't. I like rock.

questions.question: Do you have time to work?
What kind of music do you like?

blocks.content: Let's learn some activities we do on our daily routine.

blocks.title: Match the related items
blocks.instruction: Connect each item on the left to its related item on the right.

blocks.content: We can add extra information to say when or how often an activity happens.

blocks.title: Grammar Reference
blocks.explanation: Summarize the structure or contrast in a concise way.
commonMistakes: Do not forget the auxiliary verb.

comparisons.label: Compare
comparisons.detail: Use this form, not that form.

miniPractice: Write one sentence using the target structure.

blocks.title: Make 3 questions with DO + YOU and Routine Activities
blocks.instruction: Example: Do you brush you teeth?

blocks.explanation: Use a short answer first. Then you can add extra information.
blocks.details: Keep the same answer pattern every time in this part.

blocks.title: Make 3 questions with DO + YOU and Routine Activities
blocks.instruction: Example: Do you brush you teeth?

blocks.content: After saying hello, people often ask how you are. Use a short feeling word to answer.

cards.backText: Use this when you need rest.
cards.shortMeaning: Use this when you need rest.

cards.backText: Use this when you feel happy.
cards.shortMeaning: Use this when you feel happy.

cards.backText: Use this before a test, presentation, or new situation.
cards.shortMeaning: Use this before a test, presentation, or new situation.

blocks.title: Grammar Note
blocks.ruleContext: Add a short and neutral explanation of the language pattern here.

blocks.text:
A: How [] you?
B: I'm [], thanks. And []?
A: I'm fine.

blocks.title: Talking
prompts: What is your opinion on the topic?

exchanges.text: What is your opinion on the topic?

blocks.content: Now use Are you...? for identity, feelings, condition, nationality, and origin. This is a different question pattern from Do you...?.

blocks.title: Question Pattern: Are You...?
blocks.explanation: Use Are you + information to ask about a person.
blocks.details: Use this pattern for a student, a teacher, tired, happy, Brazilian, and from Brazil.

cells.text: Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.
Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.
Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.

blocks.title: Do You...? or Are You...?
blocks.explanation: Use Do you...? for actions. Use Are you...? for information about the person.
blocks.details: Look at the first words and the answer.

blocks.text:
[] you a student?
[] you tired?
[] you happy?
[] you from Brazil?

Are you a student?
Yes, I [].
Are you tired?
No, I'm [].
Are you from Brazil?
Yes, I [].

messages.speaker: Ana
messages.text: Do you study English at night?

blocks.title: Talking
prompts: What is your opinion on the topic?

exchanges.text: What is your opinion on the topic?

blocks.content: Use Are you from...? to check a country or city. Use Where are you from? when you need the place.

blocks.title: Origin Questions
blocks.explanation: Use from with a country or city.
blocks.details: Use Are you from...? for yes/no. Use Where are you from? for information.

cells.text: Yes, I am. I'm from Brazil.
No, I'm not. I'm from Brazil.

blocks.instruction: Connect each item on the left to its related item on the right.

B: Yes, I [].
A: Where are you []?

A: Are you Brazilian?

messages.speaker: Ben
messages.text: Yes, I am. I'm from Toronto.

script.text: Hello! I'm Kenji. I'm from Japan. I'm Japanese. This is Laura. She is from Mexico. She is Mexican. We study English together.

blocks.content: This interview connects greetings, Do you...?, Are you...?, and Where are you from?.

messages.speaker: Sofia
messages.text: Yes, I am. I'm from São Paulo.

blocks.title: Interview Models
blocks.explanation: Use these questions to learn basic information about another person.
blocks.details: Notice the answer pattern for each question.

blocks.text:
A: What's your []?
B: My name is Carla.
A: Are you [] Brazil?
B: Yes, I [].
A: Where are you from?
B: I'm [] São Paulo.
A: Do you [] English?
B: Yes, I [].
A: Are you a student?
B: Yes, I [].

script.text: Hi! My name is Sofia. I'm 20 years old. I'm from São Paulo, Brazil. I'm Brazilian. I'm a student. I study English at night. I like music and movies. I'm good today.
